issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: Small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG minimum 44 × 44 px touch target, making it hard for finger users to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon‑only button sets min-width and min-height to 20px, below the required 44 px minimum, reducing tap accuracy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 360: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule applies word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses word gaps and harms readability for screen‑reader users and low‑vision readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 140: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: Global rule “a { text-decoration: none; }” removes underlines from all links without providing another visual cue (e.g., color contrast, border), making links indistinguishable for users relying on visual differentiation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 180: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: .btn defines transitions (including transform) that animate on interaction, but there is no corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable them, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 300: .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); }
  Line 1240: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button in the hero section animates (transform, box‑shadow) on hover, but no reduced‑motion media query disables this animation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 720: .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); }
  Line 1245: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: Hero images animate with transform and box‑shadow on hover, but no reduced‑motion override disables this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 800: .hero-image img { transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); }
  Line 1250: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards animate (transform, box‑shadow) on hover, but lack a reduced‑motion media query to suppress the animation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 950: .feature-card { transition: border-color var(--transition-base), box-shadow var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); }
  Line 1255: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards animate (transform, box‑shadow) on hover without a reduced‑motion override, violating WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1080: .pricing-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); }
  Line 1260: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region – product filter count badge
issuesFound.explanation: When the product filter changes, the visible count of matching products is updated in the element `[data-product-count]` via `textContent`, but no `aria-live` region is used to announce the new count to screen‑reader users. This leaves users unaware of how many results are available after filtering.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region – active filter chips
issuesFound.explanation: The active filter chip list is rebuilt in `activeFilterChips.render()` after each filter change, but the changes are not announced to assistive technologies. Users cannot know when a filter has been added or removed.

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing `aria-controls` – mobile navigation button
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` the hamburger button (`_trigger`) receives `aria-expanded`, but `aria-controls` is only set if the drawer already has an `id`. If the drawer lacks an `id`, the button has no explicit relationship to the controlled navigation panel, breaking the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing `aria-controls` – dropdown menu buttons
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu.register()` the trigger button receives `aria-haspopup` and `aria-expanded`, but `aria-controls` is only added when the panel already has an `id`. If the panel lacks an `id`, the relationship is missing, preventing screen readers from linking the button to its menu.

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via live region – active filter chip removal
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a chip’s “×” button removes the filter, but no live‑region announcement is made. Users are left unaware that the filter has been cleared.
issuesFound.fix:
After removing the chip, announce the change:  
  ```js
  liveRegion.announce(`${label} filter removed`);
  ```

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region – pricing period toggle button state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`_toggleEl`) updates its visual state and announces via `liveRegion.announce`, but the button’s `aria-pressed` state is only toggled on the visual element, not on the underlying `<button>` when it is rendered as a non‑button element (e.g., a `<div>`). This can leave assistive tech without a reliable state indicator.

issuesFound.title: Missing `aria-live` for FAQ accordion open/close announcements on some browsers
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion uses `liveRegion.announce` for open/close, but on browsers that suppress `liveRegion` updates during rapid DOM changes (e.g., Safari), the announcement may be lost. The code does not provide a fallback `role="alert"` on the panel itself.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing `aria-pressed` – product filter view‑mode buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode toggle buttons (`[data-view-mode]`) change the grid layout between “grid” and “list”, but they only receive `aria-pressed` via `setAttr` inside `_onViewToggle`. If a button is rendered as a `<a>` element (common in some themes), `aria-pressed` is not appropriate; it should be `aria-selected` on a radio‑group or `aria-pressed` on a true button. The current implementation may leave the state ambiguous for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Missing `aria-live` for theme‑switcher announcements on some browsers
issuesFound.explanation: `themeSwitcher.set()` announces the theme change via `liveRegion.announce`, but the theme‑switcher UI also updates `data-theme-toggle` buttons without providing a dedicated live region for the announcement. On browsers that delay or suppress `polite` announcements, users may not hear the change.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.close()` the drawer element is hidden by removing CSS classes, but no `aria-hidden="true"` is applied. Screen‑reader users can still navigate into the hidden drawer, violating ARIA best practice for hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` after the class removals (or before the drawer is hidden) in `mobileNav.close()`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria‑hidden when opened
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.open()` the drawer is made visible by adding CSS classes, yet `aria-hidden` is never removed or set to `false`. The drawer remains exposed to assistive technologies as hidden, causing a mismatch between visual state and accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` (or `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`) after the drawer is shown in `mobileNav.open()`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button may lack aria‑controls if drawer has no id
issuesFound.explanation: In `mobileNav.init()` the code only sets `aria-controls` on the trigger when the drawer already has an `id`. If the drawer lacks an `id`, the button has no `aria-controls`, breaking the relationship between the control and the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer always has a unique `id` (e.g., `_drawer.id = _drawer.id || 'mobile-nav-drawer';`) and then set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);` unconditionally.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels lack aria‑hidden when collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.close()` the panel is hidden by adding the `hidden` attribute, but `aria-hidden` is never set to `true`. Assistive technologies may still expose the panel content after it is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. Likewise, in `faqAccordion.open()` remove the attribute or set it to `false`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu panels lack aria‑hidden when hidden/shown
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` toggle the panel’s `hidden` attribute but never update `aria-hidden`. This leaves the panel in the accessibility tree when visually hidden and vice‑versa.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance()` add `removeAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden');` and in `_closeInstance()` add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu updates visual state but does not provide any `aria-live` announcement. Users of screen readers receive no feedback that a menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: After changing `aria-expanded`, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` or `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` respectively.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel slides lack aria‑hidden when not active
issuesFound.explanation: In `testimonialsCarousel._goTo()` the slides are moved via CSS transforms, but the inactive slides never receive `aria-hidden="true"` and the active slide never has `aria-hidden="false"`. Assistive technologies will read all slides regardless of visibility.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the transform, set `setAttr(slide, 'aria-hidden', slideIndex === _current ? 'false' : 'true');` for each slide.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel period buttons lack aria‑controls linking to slides
issuesFound.explanation: The period‑selection buttons (e.g., “Monthly”, “Annual”) toggle the carousel but have no `aria-controls` attribute referencing the associated slide or carousel region. This breaks the required relationship for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Assign each button an `aria-controls` value that matches the `id` of the carousel container (e.g., `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', 'testimonials-carousel');`).

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav overlay element does not toggle aria‑hidden when shown/hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay (`_overlay`) is shown/hidden by adding/removing the `hidden` attribute, but its `aria-hidden` state is never updated. When visible, the overlay should be exposed (`aria-hidden="false"`); when hidden, it should be hidden (`aria-hidden="true"`).
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.open()` add `removeAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden');` and in `mobileNav.close()` add `setAttr(_overlay, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Wizard navigation buttons lack ARIA labeling
issuesFound.explanation: The multi‑step wizard’s “Previous” and “Next” buttons (`[data-wizard-prev]`, `[data-wizard-next]`) have no `aria-label` or `aria-controls` to convey their function or the panel they affect.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field does not set `aria-invalid` on error
issuesFound.explanation: When the newsletter email fails validation, the script only displays a status message; it never marks the `<input type="email">` with `aria-invalid="true"`. Screen‑reader users are not informed that the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
After a failed validation, add:  
```js
if (_emailEl) _emailEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');
```  
  and clear it on success.  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing `aria-hidden` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer (`_drawer`) is hidden by CSS classes but never receives `aria-hidden="true"` when closed, leaving it in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
In the `close()` function, after hiding the drawer, add:  
```js
_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count is updated in the badge, but no ARIA live announcement is made, so users of assistive technology are unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the badge, announce the new count:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} shown`);
```  

---

issuesFound.title: File‑upload component lacks live‑region updates for upload progress
issuesFound.explanation: Upload progress is shown only via a `<progress>` element; there is no `aria-live` region to announce start, completion, or errors, leaving screen‑reader users uninformed.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle button missing initial `aria-pressed` state
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle (`[data-theme-toggle]`) does not have `aria-pressed` set on page load, so its state is ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
After reading the stored preference, set the attribute on each button:  
```js
setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle')===pref));
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Carousel navigation buttons lack ARIA labeling
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel’s previous/next buttons (`[data-carousel-prev]`, `[data-carousel-next]`) have no `aria-label` or `aria-controls`, making their purpose invisible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hide/show not announced to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The header is hidden/shown based on scroll, but no live‑region announcement is made, so users may not know that navigation has disappeared.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload “remove” button missing explicit `aria-label` for some browsers
issuesFound.explanation: The remove button’s accessible name is set via `aria-label`, but the label text is generated from the file name only (`'×'`), which may be read as “times”. Provide a clearer label.
issuesFound.fix:
Change to:  
```js
removeBtn.setAttribute('aria-label', `Remove ${file.name}`);
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter view‑mode toggle buttons lack `aria-pressed` synchronization on init
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads, the view‑mode buttons (`[data-view-mode]`) may not reflect the current mode via `aria-pressed`, causing confusion for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
After determining the initial mode, set:  
```js
setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode')===_productFilterState.viewMode));
```

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (open)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function shows the mobile navigation drawer but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside mobileNav.open() after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() marks a field as invalid by calling srUtils.markInvalid() which only adds aria‑describedby and does not set aria‑invalid="true". Assistive technologies therefore do not announce the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Replace srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl); with field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); and keep the describedby linking.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not cleared from aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() marks a field as valid by calling srUtils.markValid() which removes aria‑invalid only if an error element is present; otherwise it leaves any existing aria‑invalid attribute untouched, so the field may remain announced as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); is called whenever the field passes validation, regardless of error‑element presence.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: pricing._onToggleClick() toggles the billing‑period switch but never updates aria‑pressed on the toggle button, so screen‑reader users cannot perceive the on/off state.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear)); where isYear reflects the new state.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._fetchAndRender() updates the suggestion list but does not write to an aria‑live region after the DOM change, so users of screen readers are not informed that new suggestions have appeared.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering suggestions, set _ statusEl.textContent to a brief announcement (e.g., “5 results found”) and ensure _ statusEl has aria‑live="polite".

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” function does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button when the drawer is opened.
issuesFound.explanation: Users of assistive technologies cannot know that the navigation menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open` function (after `_open = true`).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu “open” routine does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on its trigger button.
issuesFound.explanation: Screen‑reader users receive no state change indication for the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` before showing the panel.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” routine does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on the question button.
issuesFound.explanation: The accessible state of the FAQ item remains “collapsed” for AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening the item.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter subscription form validation does not set `aria‑invalid="true"` on the email field when the value is missing or malformed.
issuesFound.explanation: Assistive technologies cannot convey the error state of the email input.
issuesFound.fix: After a failed validation, call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: Contact form validation does not set `aria‑invalid="true"` on fields that fail the inline validator.
issuesFound.explanation: Users of screen readers receive no programmatic indication that a field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: For each field that fails validation, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and clear it when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions do not announce state changes via a live region.
issuesFound.explanation: AT users are not informed when a dropdown becomes visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, set `statusEl.textContent = 'Menu opened';` (with `aria-live="polite"`), and similarly announce “Menu closed” on close.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close action also lacks a live‑region announcement (separate from Issue 7 for the “close” path).
issuesFound.fix: Add a live‑region update as described in Issue 7 for the close path.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not expose a pressable state via `aria‑pressed`.
issuesFound.explanation: Users who rely on `aria‑pressed` to understand toggle buttons receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: In the `toggle`/`open`/`close` functions, also call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(_open));`.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher buttons (elements with `[data‑theme‑toggle]`) do not set `aria‑pressed` to reflect the current theme selection.
issuesFound.explanation: Screen‑reader users cannot tell which theme is active.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the theme, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle') === theme));` for each toggle button.

fpTitles: Contact form validation does not set `aria‑invalid="true"` on fields that fail the inline validator.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function in the dropdownMenu module adds the “open” class to the panel but never updates `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1582:   // after opening the panel
    Line 1583:   setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not set aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: In the `open` function of the faqAccordion module the panel is shown and ARIA attributes are set on load, but when a question is opened the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never changed to “true”, so assistive technologies cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2547:   // after expanding the item
    Line 2548:   setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateCountBadge` function in the productFilter module updates the numeric badge text but does not provide an ARIA live announcement, leaving users of assistive technology unaware of how many products match the current filters.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened or closed the UI changes (panel visibility) but no `aria-live` region is updated to inform screen‑reader users of the state change, violating dynamic content announcement requirements.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close does not set aria-expanded to false
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function correctly adds the “hidden” attribute to the panel but only sets `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger when the panel is closed via the close button; however, when the panel is closed by clicking outside or by focus loss the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute remains “true”.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1595:   // ensure trigger state is reset on any close path
    Line 1596:   setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close does not set aria-expanded to false
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in the faqAccordion module hides the answer panel but does not reset the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute to “false”, causing a mismatch between visual state and ARIA state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2559:   // after collapsing the item
    Line 2560:   setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu focus management does not move focus to the first item on open
issuesFound.explanation: After a dropdown is opened the focus remains on the trigger button; keyboard users must press ArrowDown again to reach the first menu item, which is a usability issue.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion focus is not moved to the answer panel on open
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is expanded, focus stays on the trigger button, preventing screen‑reader users from hearing the newly revealed answer without additional navigation.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not set `aria-controls` on trigger when missing
issuesFound.explanation: In the `register` function of dropdownMenu, if the trigger lacks an `aria-controls` attribute it is not added, breaking the relationship between the button and its panel for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1560:   if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId);

fpTitles: Dropdown menu focus management does not move focus to the first item on open
FAQ accordion focus is not moved to the answer panel on open

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav trigger button (`_trigger`) does not have an `aria-controls` attribute that references the `id` of the drawer panel. Without this relationship screen readers cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: After the drawer element is parsed, assign `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id)` (or generate an `id` if missing).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu click handler does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onTriggerClick` function toggles a dropdown’s visibility but never updates `aria-expanded` on the trigger button. Users of assistive technology receive no indication of the menu’s state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance`, call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` or `'false'` accordingly.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button is given `aria-haspopup` but never receives an `aria-controls` attribute that points to the associated panel’s `id`. This breaks the required relationship between control and region.
issuesFound.fix: After parsing each dropdown, ensure `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panel.id)` (and generate an `id` for the panel if absent).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.toggle()` function opens and closes FAQ items but never updates the `aria-expanded` state on the question button. Screen‑reader users cannot tell whether an answer is visible.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: While each FAQ trigger receives `aria-haspopup`, it lacks an `aria-controls` attribute that references its answer panel. This prevents assistive technologies from linking the button to the region it controls.
issuesFound.fix: When registering each FAQ item, ensure `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panel.id)` (and generate an `id` for the panel if missing).

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` module validates fields and adds visual error classes, but it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid inputs nor removes it on valid inputs. Assistive technologies cannot convey error state to users.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult`, when `!result.valid` call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; when `result.valid` call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')`.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field missing aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter subscription form validates the email field but relies on visual error messages only; it does not set `aria-invalid` on the `<input>` when the address is invalid, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the problem.
issuesFound.fix: After validation fails, call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; on success, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Contact form fields missing aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form uses custom validation but does not set `aria-invalid` on fields that fail (e.g., name, email, message). Users of assistive technology cannot detect which fields need correction.
issuesFound.fix: In the validation step for each field, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` on error and remove it on success.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates in search results are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The search module updates the suggestion list and result count but does not write to an `aria-live` region after each update. Users relying on screen readers receive no audible feedback that suggestions have changed.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering suggestions, set `_statusEl.textContent = ...` and ensure `_statusEl` has `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"` so the change is announced.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge text changes but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count badge, set `_statusEl.textContent = `${count} products shown` and ensure `_statusEl` has `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"`.

fpTitles: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-controls
Newsletter form email field missing aria‑invalid on error
Contact form fields missing aria‑invalid on validation error

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu toggle button (`[data-nav-menu] button[aria-haspopup]`) is shown/hidden by the `openInstance`/`closeInstance` functions, but they never update the button’s `aria-expanded` state. Screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the submenu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` in `_openInstance` and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` in `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge not announced
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count is updated in `_updateCountBadge()`, but the badge has no live‑region role or `aria‑live` attribute, so changes are not announced to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-live', 'polite')` (or wrap the badge in a `<span role="status">`) so updates are spoken.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field missing aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: When the newsletter email fails validation, the code only shows a visual error message; it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the `<input type="email">`. Users of assistive tech receive no indication of the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In the validation branch of `newsletterForm._onSubmit`, after detecting an invalid email, call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; on success, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Contact form fields missing aria‑invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form validates fields via `inlineValidator.validate()`, which marks fields visually but does **not** set `aria-invalid`. Consequently, screen‑reader users cannot detect which fields are invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Extend `inlineValidator.validate()` (or after each validation) to call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` when invalid and remove it when valid.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter hide/show does not manage aria‑hidden for off‑screen elements
issuesFound.explanation: When a product card is hidden via CSS class (`is-hidden`) the script does **not** set `aria-hidden="true"` on the element, leaving it in the accessibility tree despite being invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In `filterAnimation.run()` when adding the `hidden` class to cards, also call `setAttr(card, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when showing a card, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not set aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The same dropdown trigger identified in Issue 1 also fails to update `aria-expanded` when the menu opens or closes, duplicating the accessibility gap.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 1 – ensure `aria-expanded` is set appropriately in the open/close handlers.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter applyFilters does not announce filter changes
issuesFound.explanation: After applying a new filter, the UI updates the product grid but does not provide any live‑region announcement of the new result count or filter state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After `applyFilters()` finishes, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`)` (or a more descriptive message) using a hidden live region.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestions list lacks aria‑live for dynamic updates
issuesFound.explanation: The autocomplete suggestion list (`suggestionListRenderer`) updates its contents via `innerHTML` but the container does not have an `aria-live` attribute (it is hidden initially but never set to `polite` when shown), so updates are not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the `<ul>` created in `suggestionListRenderer.mount()` has `aria-live="polite"` (already set) **and** that the container’s `aria-live` is not removed when hidden; keep it present so screen readers announce new suggestions.

fpTitles: Contact form fields missing aria‑invalid on validation error

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function shows the off‑canvas drawer but never sets aria‑expanded on the trigger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in mobileNav.open and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in mobileNav.close (and consequently in mobileNav.toggle).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button that opens the navigation drawer never receives an aria‑controls reference to the drawer element, breaking the relationship required for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: After locating the drawer (`_drawer`), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden via CSS without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed it remains in the accessibility tree because no aria‑hidden or hidden attribute is set, causing focusable elements inside the drawer to be reachable.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and in mobileNav.open remove that attribute (`removeAttribute('aria-hidden')`).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function toggles the panel’s visibility but never updates the trigger button’s aria‑expanded state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s open/closed status.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and after closing, call `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown changes visible content (menu items) but no aria‑live region is updated, so users are not informed of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` and after closing, announce “Menu closed”.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing an FAQ answer changes visible content but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened || 'Answer expanded');` and after closing, announce “Answer collapsed”.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-controls attribute (already set) – but aria‑expanded still missing (covered above).
Mobile navigation trigger lacks aria‑pressed for two‑state toggle (optional) – not required by spec, so no issue.
Product filter “view‑mode” toggle button does not set aria‑pressed on state change
issuesFound.explanation: The button that switches between grid and list view toggles visual classes but never updates aria‑pressed, which is required for a two‑state toggle button.
issuesFound.fix: After changing mode, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(mode === 'list'));` for each view‑mode button.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle (annual/monthly) does not set aria‑pressed on the switch element
issuesFound.explanation: Assistive technologies expect aria‑pressed on toggle buttons; using aria‑checked may not be conveyed correctly.
issuesFound.fix: Replace `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', ...)` with `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', ...)`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce the new price via live region (already announced via liveRegion.announce in setPeriod). No issue.
Mobile navigation open/close already announces via liveRegion.announce in open/close – OK.
Sticky header hide/show does not announce state – not required (visual only).
Scroll‑to‑top button does not set aria‑pressed or aria‑expanded (not a toggle). No issue.

fpTitles: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-controls attribute (already set) – but aria‑expanded still missing (covered above).

Sticky header hide/show does not announce state – not required (visual only).
Scroll‑to‑top button does not set aria‑pressed or aria‑expanded (not a toggle). No issue.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module’s open() and close() functions add/remove CSS classes and lock scrolling, but they never set aria‑expanded on the hamburger button (_trigger). Assistive technologies therefore cannot tell whether the navigation drawer is open.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: _dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() show/hide the panel and toggle CSS classes, but they never update aria‑expanded on the button that controls the menu.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search combobox does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: _siteSearch._open() and _close() show and hide the suggestion list, but they never set aria‑expanded on the search <input>. Screen readers cannot detect the open/closed state of the combobox.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() and close() add/remove CSS classes and animate the panel, but they never set aria‑expanded on the question button. Users of assistive tech cannot know whether an answer is visible.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons (e.g., “Monthly”, “Annual”) receive a visual .active class, but they never expose their pressed state via aria‑pressed, which is required for non‑visual users.

issuesFound.title: Discount‑code input status not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: discountCodeInput._onApply() writes a message into _statusEl but the element lacks role="status" and aria‑live, so the result is not spoken by screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator never sets aria‑invalid on error fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() adds an error message element but does not set aria‑invalid on the offending field, leaving the error invisible to AT.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the code removes the error message but leaves aria‑invalid set, causing a persistent error state for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
(see Issue 7 – the removeAttr call already clears it; ensure it runs on the valid branch).  

---

issuesFound.title: Search input missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: suggestionListRenderer.mount() sets aria‑autocomplete, aria‑controls, role, aria‑haspopup but never defines the initial aria‑expanded state (should be false when the list is hidden).
issuesFound.fix:
```js
setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // ← add during mount
```  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ question buttons missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: faqRegistry.parse() creates the trigger button and sets aria‑controls but does not set aria‑expanded to false when the item is closed, so screen readers cannot know the default state.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod() updates prices and UI but never announces the change (e.g., “Annual billing selected”). Users relying on AT miss the update.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() and close() change visibility but do not provide an audible cue; AT users are unaware of the state change.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.open() and close() toggle the drawer but do not inform screen readers that the main navigation has been opened or closed.

fpTitles: FAQ accordion triggers missing aria‑expanded updates

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function shows the navigation drawer but never sets `aria‑expanded` on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is not given an `aria-controls` attribute that points to the drawer’s `id`. Assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance(inst)` adds the CSS class to show the panel but never updates `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, leaving the expanded state undisclosed to AT.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button does not have an `aria-controls` attribute that references its panel, so the relationship is not exposed to assistive tech.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open(item)` expands the answer panel but never sets `aria‑expanded` on the question button (`item.trigger`). The expanded state is therefore invisible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion panel does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `_open()` shows the suggestion list but never toggles `aria‑expanded` on the search input (`_inputEl`). Users of assistive technology cannot tell whether the list is open.

issuesFound.title: Discount‑code field validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a discount code is rejected, the input (`_inputEl`) is not marked with `aria‑invalid="true"`, so screen‑reader users receive no indication of the error state.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons (`_periodBtns`) change the pricing plan but never reflect their pressed state via `aria‑pressed`, making it impossible for AT to know which period is active.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden state lacks aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close()` hides the drawer with CSS classes but does not set `aria‑hidden="true"` on the drawer element, so the hidden region remains in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu updates the UI but provides no audible feedback for screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: User actions that show/hide UI (e.g., opening a dropdown) lack dedicated live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The UI change is perceptible visually but no `aria-live` element is updated, violating the requirement that dynamic content changes be announced.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden live‑region element (e.g., `
`) and update its text in each show/hide handler as shown in Issue 10.

fpTitles: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑controls

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance adds the open class to the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the button that controls the dropdown, leaving the expanded state undisclosed to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
In _openInstance add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in _closeInstance add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items do not set aria‑expanded on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open and faqAccordion.close toggle the visual state of an FAQ item but never set aria‑expanded on the question button, so the expanded/collapsed state is not exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In open add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in close add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

---

issuesFound.title: Search combobox does not set aria‑expanded when suggestions panel opens
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._open adds the suggestion list to the DOM but never sets aria‑expanded on the search <input>, so screen readers cannot know that a listbox is now visible.
issuesFound.fix:
In _open add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in _close add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period on/off switch does not update aria‑pressed or aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off switch that toggles between month and year updates visual state only; it never sets aria‑pressed (or aria‑expanded) on the control element, leaving its state hidden from AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In _onToggleClick add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year')); (and optionally setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true') when open).

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change is not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod updates prices on the page but does not provide any aria‑live announcement, so users of assistive technology are unaware that the billing period (and therefore the price) has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the UI, set _statusEl.textContent to a concise message (e.g., “Annual billing selected”) and ensure _statusEl has aria‑live="polite". Alternatively, call liveRegion.announce(…) with the same message.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion missing aria‑controls on question buttons (initial load)
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is parsed, the button receives aria‑haspopup and aria‑expanded but never receives aria‑controls pointing to the associated answer panel, breaking the relationship for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In faqRegistry.parse after creating panelId add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId); (and ensure the panel has the matching id).

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list items lack role="option" and aria‑selected updates on keyboard navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion list renders <li> elements with role="option", but the keyboard navigation code only calls suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex which toggles a CSS class; it does not set aria‑selected on the active option, so screen readers cannot identify the highlighted suggestion.
issuesFound.fix:
In suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex add setAttr(activeItem, 'aria-selected', 'true'); and remove aria‑selected from the previously active item.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter “view‑mode” toggle button does not set aria‑pressed or aria‑expanded when switching between grid and list views
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons toggle CSS classes but never update aria‑pressed (or aria‑expanded) to reflect the current view, leaving the state invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In _productFilter._onViewToggle after applying the view mode, add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === _currentMode)); for each view‑mode button.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden with display:none but aria‑hidden is never set, and the focus trap is not announced when opened
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile drawer is opened, focus is trapped but the drawer element itself is not marked with aria‑hidden="false" (or removed), and when closed it remains in the accessibility tree, potentially confusing AT users.
issuesFound.fix:
In open add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false'); and in close add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); (or rely on hidden attribute consistently).

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are hidden with hidden attribute but the corresponding trigger buttons lack aria‑expanded and aria‑controls initially (page load)
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger buttons are given aria‑haspopup but never receive aria‑expanded or aria‑controls on page load, violating the requirement that the collapsed state be expressed in markup.
issuesFound.fix: During register add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); and setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId); where panelId is the id of the associated panel.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on newsletter email input
issuesFound.explanation: The `<TextInput>` for the newsletter email includes `aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error"` but no element with that id exists in the component, so screen readers receive a reference to a non‑existent element. This prevents the error message from being announced.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact name input
issuesFound.explanation: The `<TextInput>` for “Full name” sets `aria-describedby="contact-name-error"` but no element with that id is ever rendered, breaking the reference and preventing the error message from being announced.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact email input
issuesFound.explanation: The email `<TextInput>` uses `aria-describedby="contact-email-error"` but no element with that id exists, so assistive technologies cannot associate the error message with the field.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact subject input
issuesFound.explanation: The subject `<TextInput>` references `aria-describedby="contact-subject-error"` but no such element is rendered, breaking the association.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact message textarea
issuesFound.explanation: The message `<Textarea>` includes `aria-describedby="contact-message-error"` (and also the character‑count id) but no element with `contact-message-error` is rendered, breaking the reference.

issuesFound.title: Loading button does not expose loading state (NewsletterForm)
issuesFound.explanation: The `<Button>` in `NewsletterForm` receives a prop `loading={isSubmitting}` but the `Button` component expects `isLoading`. Consequently `aria‑busy` is never set and the loading state is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Pass the correct prop name `isLoading`.  
  Fix:  
  Line 1110:    <Button … loading={isSubmitting} …> → <Button … isLoading={isSubmitting} …>

issuesFound.title: Loading button does not expose loading state (ContactForm)
issuesFound.explanation: The `<Button>` in `ContactForm` also uses the incorrect prop `loading={isSubmitting}` instead of `isLoading`, so `aria‑busy` is omitted and the loading state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
Use the correct prop name.  
  Fix:  
  Line 1235:    <Button … loading={isSubmitting} …> → <Button … isLoading={isSubmitting} …>

issuesFound.title: Clickable backdrop div lacks button semantics (MobileNav)
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop `<div>` in `MobileNav` has an `onClick` handler to close the navigation but is a plain `<div>` without `role="button"` or `tabIndex`, making it inaccessible to keyboard users and screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `role="button"` and `tabIndex={0}` (and optionally an `onKeyDown` handler for Enter/Space).  
  Fix:  
  Line 845:    <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" aria-hidden="true" onClick={onClose}> → <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-hidden="true" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e)=>{if(e.key==="Enter"||e.key===" "){e.preventDefault();onClose();}}}>

issuesFound.title: Clickable overlay div lacks button semantics (Modal)
issuesFound.explanation: The outer `<div className="modal-overlay">` in `Modal` has an `onClick` handler to close the dialog but is a non‑interactive `<div>` without `role="button"` or `tabIndex`, preventing keyboard users from activating it.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `role="button"` and `tabIndex={0}` (and an `onKeyDown` handler).  
  Fix:  
  Line 1662:    <div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose}> → <div className="modal-overlay" role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e)=>{if(e.key==="Enter"||e.key===" "){e.preventDefault();onClose();}}}>

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on NewsletterForm email input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> in NewsletterForm has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error", but no element with that id exists in the rendered markup, so screen‑readers cannot locate the associated error message. This blocks users from understanding validation feedback.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a matching ErrorMessage element (or change the aria-describedby value).  
  ```
  Line 617: <TextInput
  Line 618:   id="newsletter-email"
  Line 619:   type="email"
  Line 620:   name="email"
  Line 621:   placeholder="you@company.com"
  Line 622:   aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? "newsletter-email-error" : undefined}
  Line 623:   ... />
  Line 624: <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.error} />
  ```

  ```
  Line 1031:   ... />
  ```


issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria‑busy in NewsletterForm submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> used for the newsletter submit passes a loading prop, but the Button component expects isLoading. Consequently aria‑busy is never set and the loading state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Rename the prop to isLoading so the component applies aria‑busy.  
  ```
  Line 630: <Button
  Line 631:   type="submit"
  Line 632:   variant="primary"
  Line 633:   size="md"
  Line 634:   isLoading={isSubmitting}   // changed from loading={isSubmitting}
  Line 635:   aria-disabled={isSubmitting}
  Line 636:   aria-describedby={statusId}
  Line 637: >
  ```

issuesFound.fix:
Change the prop to isLoading.  
  ```
  Line 1150: <Button
  Line 1151:   type="submit"
  Line 1152:   variant="primary"
  Line 1153:   size="md"
  Line 1154:   isLoading={isSubmitting}   // changed from loading={isSubmitting}
  Line 1155:   aria-disabled={isSubmitting}
  Line 1156: >
  ```

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria-describedby references missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for the newsletter email sets aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" when an error exists, but no element with that ID is ever rendered, breaking the reference for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1245: `<TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? undefined : undefined} />`  
  *(remove the conditional aria-describedby or render an element with the matching ID)*

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Full name” input aria-describedby references missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Full name” includes aria-describedby="contact-name-error" when an error is present, but no element with that ID is rendered, breaking the ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1305: `<TextInput id="contact-name" name="name" type="text" autoComplete="name" required aria-required="true" value={name.state.value} onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={name.onBlur} invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error} aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? undefined : undefined} />`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Email address” input aria-describedby references missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The email field’s aria-describedby points to contact-email-error, which is never rendered, so AT users cannot access the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1325: `<TextInput id="contact-email" name="email" type="email" autoComplete="email" required aria-required="true" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? undefined : undefined} />`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Subject” input aria-describedby references missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The subject field’s aria-describedby references contact-subject-error, which does not exist in the DOM, breaking the link for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1345: `<TextInput id="contact-subject" name="subject" type="text" required aria-required="true" value={subject.state.value} onChange={(ev) => subject.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={subject.onBlur} invalid={subject.state.touched && !!subject.state.error} aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? undefined : undefined} />`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Message” textarea aria-describedby references missing element
issuesFound.explanation: The textarea’s aria-describedby includes contact-message-error when an error exists, but no element with that ID is ever rendered, breaking the reference for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1365: `<Textarea id="contact-message" name="message" rows={6} required aria-required="true" value={message.state.value} onChange={(ev) => message.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={message.onBlur} invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error} aria-describedby={[\`contact-message-counter\`].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} />`  
  *(remove the missing error ID from aria-describedby*)

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit button missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The submit <Button> receives a loading prop (not the expected isLoading), so aria-busy is never set. Assistive technologies therefore cannot know the button is busy.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1260: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>`  
  *(replace loading with isLoading or add aria-busy={isSubmitting})*

issuesFound.title: ContactForm submit button missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form’s submit <Button> uses a loading prop instead of the component’s isLoading prop, so aria-busy is not applied and AT users cannot detect the loading state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1385: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}>`  
  *(use isLoading or add aria-busy={isSubmitting})*

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. An outline-offset applied to a zero-width outline renders nothing visible, removing the keyboard focus indicator entirely. This blocks keyboard-only users from identifying which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, clip: auto, and overflow: visible. This does NOT hide content visually—it leaves it fully visible on screen. A correct sr-only implementation requires position: absolute, 1px dimensions, and clip: rect(0,0,0,0) to hide from sighted users while remaining accessible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links lack non-colour visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The rule footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from links. The :hover state at line 1019 only changes colour (to var(--colour-neutral-0)) without adding an underline, border, or other non-colour cue. Users who cannot perceive colour differences cannot distinguish these links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the 44px minimum recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments or on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion media query provides no motion reduction
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 509 is completely empty with no selectors or properties. issuesFound.title: No forced-colors/high-contrast-mode override for interactive element colours

Interactive elements themselves lack forced-colors overrides, causing them to potentially become invisible in Windows High Contrast Mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like a border or box-shadow in the same rule block. issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. outline-offset does not create a visible ring if the outline width is 0. This removes keyboard focus indication entirely.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block sets outline: none on :focus-visible. This ensures the focus ring is invisible in Windows High Contrast Mode, blocking access for users relying on this mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector relies on the global :focus-visible rule which sets outline: none. It only adjusts the offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector sets outline-color but inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule. Without outline-style or outline-width, the color property has no effect, hiding the focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus style sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users interacting with buttons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button focus style sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links have outline: none with only outline-offset. Keyboard users cannot see which menu item is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input focus style uses box-shadow as the only visible indicator after outline: none. Box-shadows are suppressed in Windows High Contrast Mode, making the focus invisible for those users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible state for search input sets outline: none with no visible replacement, hiding the focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button focus style sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links have outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the visible focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs use box-shadow as the only visible indicator after outline: none. Box-shadows are invisible in Windows High Contrast Mode.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static with width: auto and height: auto, which does not visually hide content. A proper sr-only utility requires position: absolute, 1px dimensions, and clip to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This broken implementation fails to hide content visually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 453: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) with transition: transform, causing spatial movement. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 503 is empty and does not override this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 503: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform and .btn:active uses transform: translateY(1px), causing spatial movement. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists for these button animations. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see this transform animation.

Line 533: .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }
  Line 547: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

  Line 591: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img uses transform: perspective() rotateY() rotateX() with transition: transform, causing significant spatial movement. issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus ring for all elements, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 333: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, :focus-visible has outline: none with no replacement. This ensures the focus ring is invisible specifically when users need high-contrast mode the most.
issuesFound.fix: Line 345: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not set aria-hidden="false" on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds the visible class to the drawer element but fails to set aria-hidden="false". Sighted users see the drawer, but screen reader users may still perceive it as hidden if aria-hidden remains true or unset from initialization.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1385: addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function updates aria-expanded and shows the panel but does not announce the menu opening to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on AT are not informed that the dropdown has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1553: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); requestAnimationFrame(() => addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open)); liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function updates aria-expanded and hides the panel but does not announce the menu closing to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on AT are not informed that the dropdown has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1572: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open); liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._showBanner function does not announce banner appearance to live region

issuesFound.fix: Line 4139: _store(ACCEPTED); analytics.grantConsent(); _hideBanner(); liveRegion.announce('Cookie preferences accepted');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._onDecline function does not announce decision to live region

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions within the `dropdownMenu` module change the visibility of dropdown panels and update `aria-expanded` states, but they do not write to an `aria-live` region. Screen reader users are not informed when a dropdown menu opens or closes, which degrades navigation awareness.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1735: _openInstance(inst); liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');
  Line 1760: _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus); liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader visibility changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onScroll` function in the `stickyHeader` module toggles the header's visibility (hide/show) based on scroll direction and emits custom events, but it does not announce this state change to an `aria-live` region. Users relying on screen readers are not notified when the navigation header disappears or reappears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2005: if (movingDown && !_hidden) { ... emit(_header, 'header:hide'); liveRegion.announce('Header hidden'); } else if (!movingDown && _hidden) { ... emit(_header, 'header:show'); liveRegion.announce('Header shown'); }

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner display not announced to screen readers

Screen reader users may not be immediately aware that a consent dialog has appeared if focus management is delayed or bypassed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3735: document.body.appendChild(_bannerEl); ... liveRegion.announce('Cookie consent settings available');

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button aria-pressed not initialised on load
issuesFound.explanation: In the `testimonialsCarousel.init` function, the `play()` function (which sets `aria-pressed='true'` on `_playBtn`) is only called if `autoPlay` is enabled. If `autoPlay` is disabled, `aria-pressed` is never initialised to `'false'` on the play button, leaving its state ambiguous to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3545: if ((CONFIG.carousel ? CONFIG.carousel.autoPlay !== false : true) && !prefersReducedMotion()) { play(); } else { pause(); }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the _trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is currently open or closed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav dynamically-hidden drawer not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle CSS classes but do not manage the aria-hidden attribute on the _drawer element. While CSS may hide it visually, the accessibility tree may still expose it without explicit aria-hidden management.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates in dropdownMenu not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes visible content but does not announce the state change via a live region. Screen reader users are not notified when a menu opens or closes unexpectedly.

issuesFound.title: productFilter toggle buttons not updating aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._syncTabAriaStates function updates the CSS active class on filter tabs but does not update the aria-pressed attribute. Users relying on assistive technology cannot identify which filter is currently active.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates in productFilter not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates in faqAccordion not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive elements missing keyboard handler in themeSwitcher

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive elements missing keyboard handler in scrollToTop
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop.init function attaches a click listener to the _btn element but does not attach a keydown listener for Enter or Space keys. If the button is not a native <button> element, keyboard users cannot activate it.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load in siteSearch
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init function mounts the suggestion listbox but does not explicitly set aria-expanded="false" on the input element initially. The attribute is only set when show() or hide() is called later.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `<section>` element serving as the page hero lacks an accessible name (`aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`). Although it contains an `h1` with `id="hero-heading"`, the section does not reference it, making navigation via landmarks ambiguous.

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar duplicate list missing aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs toggle buttons missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #search-input:focus sets outline: none and replaces it with a box-shadow focus ring. Box-shadow is invisible in Windows High Contrast Mode (Forced Colors), leaving mouse users with no visible focus indicator. The :focus-visible state provides an outline, but :focus (triggered by mouse clicks) relies solely on the non-high-contrast-compatible box-shadow.
issuesFound.fix: Line 730: Replace box-shadow with an outline declaration that is visible in high contrast mode, or remove the outline: none declaration from the :focus rule and rely on the :focus-visible outline which already exists at line 734.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width of 20px and height of 20px, which is below the 24×24 px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1547: Increase dimensions to at least 24px, or add an invisible label or wrapper that extends the clickable area to 24×24 px while keeping the visual checkbox at its current size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit dimensions of 28px × 28px with zero padding, falling below the recommended 44×44 px target size. While it meets the absolute minimum of 24px, the small size may still cause difficulty for users with tremors or limited precision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 520: Change width and height to 44px; or Line 740: Add padding to extend the clickable area to at least 44×44 px total.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 36px with vertical padding of 6px (var(--space-1-5)), resulting in a total height of 36px. This is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended target size for touch inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 615: Increase min-height to 44px, or add additional transparent padding to ensure the total clickable area is at least 44px in height.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links set min-height: 24px with no compensating padding, meeting the minimum size but falling short of the recommended 44px target. This makes the links difficult to activate for users with motor disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1579: Increase min-height to 44px, or add vertical padding to ensure the total clickable area reaches 44px in height.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links remove the default underline with text-decoration: none and provide only a colour change on hover (line 534). Colour alone is not sufficient to distinguish links from surrounding text for users with low vision or colour blindness (SC 1.4.1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 534: Add text-decoration: underline; or add a border-bottom: 1px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); to the hover state, or increase font-weight on hover to provide a non-colour visual differentiator.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links remove the default underline with text-decoration: none and rely solely on a colour change (to var(--colour-neutral-0)) on hover (line 1581). This fails WCAG 1.4.1 as colour alone cannot be perceived by all users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1581: Add text-decoration: underline; or border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; to provide a non-colour visual indicator that the text is a link.
