issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links (nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a) remove the default text decoration (underline) and rely solely on color change on hover to indicate interactivity. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 as color alone is not sufficient to distinguish links from surrounding text for users with color vision deficiencies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 673: Add a non-color visual differentiator such as underline, border-bottom, or font-weight change. Example: Add border-bottom: 1px solid transparent; and on hover (Line 676) add border-bottom: 1px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); or add text-decoration: underline; to the hover state.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative: footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links remove the default text decoration and rely solely on color change (to var(--colour-neutral-0)) on hover. Without an additional visual cue such as underline, border, or font-weight change, users cannot distinguish these links from plain text if they cannot perceive the color change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1396: Add text-decoration: underline; or add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; to provide a non-color visual indicator. Alternatively, add font-weight: var(--font-weight-bold); on hover (Line 1394) to create a visible distinction.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative: social links
issuesFound.explanation: The social media links (.social-links a) remove text decoration and rely solely on color change on hover to indicate they are interactive. This creates a barrier for users who cannot perceive color changes, as there is no underline, border, or other visual differentiator to distinguish the links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1406: Add text-decoration: underline; or border-bottom: 1px solid transparent; with border-bottom-color: currentColor; on hover (Line 1411) to ensure links are distinguishable without relying solely on color.

issuesFound.title: Global focus-visible indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px, which renders no visible focus ring since the outline width is zero. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard users navigating buttons.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox input touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] has explicit width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link touch target may be below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a has min-height: 24px, which meets the minimum but is below the 44px recommended size for comfortable touch activation, and has no compensating padding in the rule.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which keeps content visible in the layout instead of hiding it for screen-reader-only announcement.

issuesFound.title: Button transition lacks prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transform var(--transition-fast)), which causes spatial movement animation not covered by any @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule.

issuesFound.title: Hero CTA button transition lacks prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes transform in its transition property, causing spatial movement that is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Skip link transition lacks prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class has transition: transform var(--transition-base), which animates the transform property without a prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable it.

issuesFound.title: Featured pricing card hover transform lacks motion reduction support
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01), which is spatial movement not covered by the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block that only targets .pricing-card:hover.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing on headings impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The h1-h6 selector uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) which is defined as -0.025em, reducing readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing on hero heading
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-content h1 uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tighter) (-0.05em), which is excessively tight and impairs readability.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing on section headers
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple section heading selectors use letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) (-0.025em), reducing text readability.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing on card headings
issuesFound.explanation: Card heading selectors (.feature-card h3, .step-content h3, .product-body h3, .pricing-card h3) use negative letter-spacing values that reduce readability.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing on price amount
issuesFound.explanation: The .price-amount uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tighter) (-0.05em), making the price text harder to read.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation links lack non-color visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector sets text-decoration: none and only changes color on hover, failing to provide a non-color visual indicator for links (WCAG 1.4.1).

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring. No box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline is provided in this rule or a paired base rule to replace the removed default focus indicator. Keyboard users will not see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. In high-contrast modes, box-shadow is suppressed, so outline is the only reliable focus indicator. Removing it without replacement makes focus invisible to Windows High Contrast users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible selector only sets outline-offset: -3px but does not set a visible outline style. Since the global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none, this results in no visible focus indicator for elements using this class.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible selector only sets outline-color but does not set outline-width or style. Combined with the global outline: none, this results in no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow or border.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu links set outline: none with only outline-offset: -3px, providing no visible alternative focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. The vertical padding of 6px (var(--space-1-5)) is also less than 8px, resulting in an insufficient touch target size for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon button sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with tremors or limited fine motor control.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search button sets width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding. While this meets the 24px minimum, it is below the 44px recommended size and lacks compensating padding of at least 8px on each side, potentially causing difficulty for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. This makes the checkbox difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition properties, causing spatial movement on state changes. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link class includes a transform transition for its slide-in animation. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA primary button includes transform in its transition properties (transform var(--transition-fast)), causing spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image includes transform in its transition (transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower)), causing 3D perspective rotation on hover. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 662 is empty and does not disable this transition.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: The product card includes transform in its transition (transform var(--transition-base)), causing translateY movement on hover. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it. Additionally, it lacks the proper absolute positioning and clipping techniques needed to visually hide content while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks accessibility for skip links and screen-reader-only labels.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or low vision. Negative word-spacing can cause words to run together visually.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px, which creates no visible focus ring because the outline width is zero. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the outline is removed without replacement. In Windows High Contrast Mode, box-shadow is suppressed, leaving users with no focus indicator at all.
issuesFound.fix: Line 369: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required for touch targets. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these small buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: search submit button 28px

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: checkbox input 20px

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer nav links min-height 24px

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class incorrectly uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 381: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn transform transition
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition properties (line 494) but has no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 500: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary transform transition
issuesFound.explanation: This button has a transform transition (line 809) without a reduced-motion override, which can cause issues for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img transform transition
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transform transition for hover effects (line 891) without a reduced-motion override to respect user preferences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 891: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335: body { ... word-spacing: normal; ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: skip-link foreground/background
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link uses background-color #5588bb (var(--colour-brand-primary)) against white text (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.9:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (16px font).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: footer nav links foreground/background
issuesFound.explanation: Footer links use color #94a3b8 (var(--colour-neutral-400)) against background #555555 (var(--colour-neutral-900)), resulting in approximately 2.9:1 contrast, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: social links foreground/background
issuesFound.explanation: Social links use color #94a3b8 against background #555555, the same low-contrast combination as footer nav links, resulting in approximately 2.9:1 contrast.

issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator in the default state, causing links to be distinguished from surrounding text only by colour, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with colour vision deficiencies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 114: a {\n  text-decoration: underline;\n}

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle buttons missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher toggle buttons (selected via [data-theme-toggle]) do not receive an initial aria-pressed attribute when the page loads. The code only sets aria-pressed when a button is clicked via the set() function, leaving screen reader users unable to determine the current active state on initial page load.

issuesFound.title: Carousel play button missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When the testimonials carousel initializes with autoplay disabled (CONFIG.carousel.autoPlay === false) or when the user prefers reduced motion, the play/pause button (_playBtn) lacks an initial aria-pressed attribute. The attribute is only populated when the play() or pause() functions are subsequently called.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-controls if drawer lacks id
issuesFound.explanation: The code conditionally sets aria-controls on the mobile navigation toggle button only if the drawer element already possesses an id attribute. If the drawer element exists in the DOM but lacks an id, the aria-controls relationship is never established, preventing screen reader users from identifying which element the button controls.

fpTitles: Theme toggle buttons missing aria-pressed initialization
Carousel play button missing aria-pressed initialization
Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-controls if drawer lacks id

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When the discount code validation fails, the function announces the error via liveRegion but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input field. Screen reader users may not know the field is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3767: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `liveRegion.announceAssertive(result.message || 'Invalid code');` and add `removeAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid');` when the code is valid (before disabling the input).

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When file validation fails (size or type), the error is announced but aria-invalid is not set on the file input element. Users may not perceive the error state of the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3895: Add `setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` when validation fails. Add `removeAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid');` when a valid file is added or when the field is cleared.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer is hidden via CSS class removal but may remain in the accessibility tree if CSS uses transform/translate instead of display:none. The code does not set aria-hidden="true" when the drawer is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2845: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` in the close function. Line 2820 (in open function): Add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` or `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` when opening.

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the sticky header is hidden on scroll down, it is only hidden via CSS class. If the CSS positions it off-screen without display:none, it remains accessible to screen readers. The code should set aria-hidden="true" when hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3025: Add `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` in the hide function. Line 3010 (in show function): Add `removeAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden');` when showing.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner focus management missing when closing
issuesFound.explanation: When the cookie consent banner is dismissed, the DOM element is removed without moving focus to a sensible location (e.g., the main content or the button that opened the banner). Keyboard focus is lost to the document root.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4232: Before `fadeOut`, add focus management: `const returnFocus = document.activeElement === _bannerEl ? qs('main') || document.body : document.activeElement;` and in the fadeOut callback: `if(returnFocus) returnFocus.focus();`.

issuesFound.title: contactForm missing autocomplete attributes on personal data fields
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields for full name and email address (personal data) do not have autocomplete attributes set, failing WCAG 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3545: After `if (field) inlineValidator.bind(field, cfg.rules);`, add:
  Line 3546: `if (cfg.selector.includes('full_name')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name');`
  Line 3547: `if (cfg.selector.includes('email')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm missing autocomplete attribute on email field
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input field does not have autocomplete="email" set, failing WCAG 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3482: After `if (_emailEl) {`, add `if (!_emailEl.getAttribute('autocomplete')) _emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table toggle missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module manages the comparison table visibility via setComparisonTableExpanded(), which correctly toggles aria-expanded on the trigger button. However, during initialization (pricing.init()), this function is never invoked to set the initial aria-expanded="false" state. If the comparison table starts collapsed (as implied by the hidden attribute check), screen reader users will not perceive the trigger button as controlling expandable content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2698: Add initialization call for the comparison table trigger:
  Line 2698: if (qs('[data-expand-comparison]')) setComparisonTableExpanded(false, false);

issuesFound.title: Sticky header visibility changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The sticky header hides when scrolling down and reappears when scrolling up, which is a significant layout change. The JavaScript updates CSS classes to animate this change but does not announce the visibility state change via a live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that navigation has disappeared or reappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2091: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.headerHidden || 'Navigation header hidden');
  Line 2096: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.headerShown || 'Navigation header shown');

issuesFound.title: View mode toggle buttons may lack accessibility semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter module delegates click handling to elements with [data-view-mode] attributes to toggle between grid and list views. If these elements are not native <button> elements in the HTML markup, they will lack keyboard operability and will not be recognized as interactive by assistive technologies. The JavaScript does not enforce role="button" or tabindex="0" on these delegated targets.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the HTML uses native <button> elements for [data-view-mode], or add defensive checks in the delegate handler to set role and tabindex if missing:
  Line 2361: Before processing, ensure target has role="button" and tabindex="0" if not a native button.

issuesFound.title: Wizard progress bar role not enforced
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step wizard assumes the progress bar element ([data-wizard-progress]) has role="progressbar" defined in the HTML. The JavaScript updates aria-valuenow and aria-valuetext, but if the HTML lacks the role attribute, screen readers will not recognize this as a progress indicator.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2896: Add role check and assignment:
  Line 2896: if (_progressBar && !_progressBar.getAttribute('role')) _progressBar.setAttribute('role', 'progressbar');

issuesFound.title: Comparison table trigger missing aria-controls initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-controls attribute, which creates the programmatic relationship between the comparison table trigger button and the table region, is only set when setComparisonTableExpanded() is called. During initial page load, if this function is not invoked, the trigger lacks aria-controls, preventing screen reader users from understanding what element the button controls.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2698: Add initialization for aria-controls:
  Line 2698: const compTrigger = qs('[data-expand-comparison]'); const compTable = qs('[data-pricing-comparison]'); if (compTrigger && compTable && compTable.id) setAttr(compTrigger, 'aria-controls', compTable.id);

issuesFound.title: File upload drop zone missing aria-expanded for upload list
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload drop zone acts as a disclosure widget where the selected files list expands/collapses as files are added. While the zone has role="button" and handles keyboard activation, the dynamic showing/hiding of the file list (when files are added/removed) is not accompanied by an aria-expanded state change on the trigger, nor is the list's visibility change announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3150: When files are added and the list becomes visible, set aria-expanded="true" on the zone element; set to "false" when the last file is removed and the list hides.

issuesFound.title: Media player controller missing aria-pressed for custom mute button
issuesFound.explanation: The media player controller handles the 'm' key to toggle mute state on the native media element. While this updates the native muted property, if there is a custom mute button in the UI (separate from native controls), it would need aria-pressed synchronization. The current code does not target or update any custom button's aria-pressed state, potentially causing a mismatch between the announced state and the actual state.
issuesFound.fix: If a custom mute button exists (e.g., [data-media-mute]), query it and toggle aria-pressed to match media.muted state when the 'm' key is handled or when the media's volumechange event fires.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open function reveals the mobile navigation drawer by adding CSS classes but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1323: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". This prevents screen reader users from knowing the dropdown is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1467: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid form field. Screen readers will not identify the field as having invalid input, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1027: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` before or after the `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` call

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is declared with a focus trap variable (_trap) but the trap is never initialized or activated when the menu opens. Keyboard focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1330: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { initialFocus: _drawer, returnFocus: _trigger });` and `_trap.activate();` after `_lockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the navigation drawer or the first focusable element within it. Keyboard users must manually navigate into the newly opened content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1331: Add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();` after opening the drawer

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible content but does not announce the state change to screen reader users via a live region, violating WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1470: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown opened');` and Line 1485: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown closed');`

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField focus loss on file removal
issuesFound.explanation: When a file is removed using the remove button, the button element is removed from the DOM but focus is not programmatically moved to a logical location (the upload zone or remaining files), causing focus loss for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3627: Add `binding.zoneEl.focus();` or `const remaining = binding.entries[0]; if (remaining) remaining.rowEl.querySelector('button').focus();` after `binding.entries.splice(idx, 1);` and before the emit call

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult function calls srUtils.markInvalid which does not set aria-invalid="true". Additionally, when validation passes, it should explicitly set aria-invalid="false" rather than just removing the attribute to ensure clear state communication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3158: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the else block before `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);` and Line 3162: Change to `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` instead of `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel autoplay missing pause on visibilitychange
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel auto-plays but does not pause when the document visibility changes to hidden (tab switch), causing unnecessary background processing and potential accessibility issues for users who return to the tab.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3020: Add `document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', () => { if (document.hidden) pause(); else if (CONFIG.carousel.autoPlay !== false) play(); });` in the init function

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard progress bar missing aria-valuetext update
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar updates aria-valuenow but does not consistently update aria-valuetext with the current step information in a format that screen readers can announce properly during navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3380: Ensure `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuetext', \`Step \${stepIndex + 1} of \${_steps.length}: \${_steps[stepIndex].title}\`);` is called whenever the step changes

issuesFound.title: contactForm error summary missing aria-live
issuesFound.explanation: The error summary container is created with role="alert" but does not have aria-live="assertive" explicitly set, and focus is moved to it but the announcement might not be sufficiently prioritized for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3458: Add `_errorSummaryEl.setAttribute('aria-live', 'assertive');` when creating the error summary element

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestionList missing aria-activedescendant clear
issuesFound.explanation: When the suggestion list is hidden, the aria-activedescendant attribute is removed from the input, but when no suggestion is selected while the list is open, the attribute should be managed to ensure screen readers track the highlighted option correctly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1925: Ensure `setActiveIndex(-1)` is called in the hide function to properly clear aria-activedescendant, and verify that `_inputEl.removeAttribute('aria-activedescendant');` is present in the hide function (add if missing)

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons update aria-selected but do not have aria-controls pointing to the pricing cards container, making the relationship between the toggle and the affected content unclear to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2430: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', 'pricing-cards-container');` when initializing the period buttons, assuming the container has id="pricing-cards-container"

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion search input missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ search input is linked to the container via aria-controls in the init function, but if the container does not have an id initially, the aria-controls reference may be broken.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2954: Ensure `const containerId = srUtils.ensureId(_containerEl, 'faq-list');` is called before `setAttr(_searchInputEl, 'aria-controls', containerId);` to guarantee the container has an id

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm submit button missing aria-disabled
issuesFound.explanation: During form submission, the submit button is disabled via the disabled attribute, but aria-disabled is not set to "true", which can cause some screen readers to stop announcing the button entirely rather than announcing it as unavailable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3335: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-disabled', 'true');` when disabling the button during submission, and `btn.removeAttribute('aria-disabled');` when re-enabling

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is a toggle that shows/hides based on scroll position, but it does not use aria-pressed or aria-expanded to indicate its visible state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1648: Add `setAttr(_btn, 'aria-pressed', String(_visible));` in the _update function to reflect the visible state

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher buttons missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: Theme toggle buttons update the theme but do not have aria-controls pointing to the document root or body element that is affected by the theme change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4225: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', 'document-root');` or reference the html element id to indicate what element is controlled by the theme switch

issuesFound.title: productFilter view mode toggle missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The grid/list view toggle buttons set aria-pressed but do not have aria-controls pointing to the product grid container that is affected by the view mode change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2707: Add `setAttr(b, 'aria-controls', _gridEl.id || 'product-grid');` when updating the view mode buttons

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner missing aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner dialog has aria-labelledby pointing to the title, but it does not have aria-describedby pointing to the description paragraph, which would provide better context for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4098: Add `setAttr(_bannerEl, 'aria-describedby', 'cookie-banner-desc');` assuming the description has id="cookie-banner-desc"

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module sets the _open state and CSS classes but fails to set the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen readers will not announce that the navigation menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3761: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function reveals the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4023: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function expands the FAQ answer panel but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, preventing assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6443: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility function adds an error description via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5092: Insert `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the existing `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` line within the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function hides the mobile navigation drawer but does not announce the state change to screen readers via the liveRegion announcer, leaving users unaware that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3803: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` immediately before the closing brace of the function (before the final `}`).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu reveals new content but does not announce the action via a live region, so screen reader users are not informed that the menu has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4027: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown menu opened');` immediately before the closing brace of the _openInstance function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu removes content from view but does not announce the closure via a live region, leaving users uncertain whether the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4048: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown menu closed');` immediately before the closing brace of the _closeInstance function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to activate focus trap and move focus
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function creates a focus trap instance but never calls _trap.activate(), and it does not programmatically move focus into the navigation drawer. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button while the drawer opens, violating focus management requirements for modal dialogs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3768: Insert `_trap.activate();` immediately after the line `_lockScroll();` and before the `emit()` call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion._onKeydown() missing Enter/Space handler for non-button triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The keyboard handler for FAQ items handles Arrow keys but does not include cases for Enter or Space keys. When the trigger is not a native <button> or <summary> element (e.g., a <div> with role="button"), keyboard users cannot activate the accordion.

issuesFound.title: toastManager.show() auto-dismisses without user-controllable pause
issuesFound.explanation: The toast notification uses setTimeout to automatically dismiss after a duration, but provides no mechanism for users to pause, stop, or hide this auto-updating content. This fails WCAG 2.2.1 Pause, Stop, Hide requirements.

fpTitles: faqAccordion._onKeydown() missing Enter/Space handler for non-button triggers
toastManager.show() auto-dismisses without user-controllable pause

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding an open class, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2576: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `requestAnimationFrame(() => addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open));`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the answer panel and adds visual state classes but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The attribute is only set to "false" when closing, causing a mismatch between the visual state and the accessibility API.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4230: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() helper links the error message to the field via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid input. Screen reader users rely on aria-invalid to know that a field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1896: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu controller opens and closes panels in response to user actions (click, keydown) but does not write to an aria-live region to announce these state changes. Screen reader users are not informed when a dropdown opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2579: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` inside _openInstance after setting aria-expanded. Line 2599: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` inside _closeInstance after setting aria-expanded to false.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation focus trap never created or activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable and references it in close(), but the createFocusTrap() factory is never called in init() and activate() is never called in open(). Consequently, keyboard focus is not trapped within the mobile menu when it opens, and focus is not returned to the trigger button when it closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2402: After `_initialised = true;`, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`. Line 2422: After `_lockScroll();`, add `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels by adding/removing CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but fails to update aria-expanded on the trigger buttons (inst.trigger). This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether dropdown menus are open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1456: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
    Line 1483: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `if (!inst.open) inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open() function expands answer panels and removes the hidden attribute, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). While the close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", this mismatch means screen readers announce the wrong state when items are expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2206: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the markInvalid function adds error messages to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot programmatically identify which fields contain errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 804: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation passes, the markValid function removes error references from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute. Fields remain marked as invalid to screen readers even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 819: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` before the closing brace of the function

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button lacks an aria-controls attribute linking it to the drawer element (_drawer). Screen readers cannot identify which element is controlled by the toggle button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1313: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));` immediately after setting aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer lacks focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the code creates a focus trap instance (_trap) but never activates it. Keyboard users can tab outside the drawer into the underlying page content, violating modal dialog focus containment requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1345: Add `_trap.activate();` immediately after `_lockScroll();` and before the emit call

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order and must navigate back to the trigger manually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1395: Add `_trigger.focus();` immediately after `_unlockScroll();` and before the emit call

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users apply filters to the product grid, the visible content changes and result counts update, but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not informed how many products match their selection or that the grid has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2005: Add `liveRegion.announce(\`Showing ${totalMatches} products\`);` inside the filterAnimation.run callback after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce price changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods (monthly/annual), prices update visually across all plan cards, but screen readers receive no announcement of the change. Users may not realize prices have changed or what the new amounts are.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2118: Add `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to ${period} billing\`);` after the forEach loop that updates plan cards and before the requestAnimationFrame call

fpTitles: mobileNav close does not return focus to trigger

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open() and close() functions toggle CSS classes to show/hide the drawer but never update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce whether the navigation is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1780 (in open()): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
    Line 1800 (in close()): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels by removing/adding the hidden attribute and toggling CSS classes, but fails to synchronise aria-expanded on the trigger button. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1890 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
    Line 1905 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` at the start of the function before the animation timeout

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the code manages CSS classes and animation but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an asymmetric state where opened items report as collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2835 (in open()): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1280 (in markInvalid): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` before the closing brace

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes aria-describedby references but leaves aria-invalid="true" set on fields that have been corrected. The attribute should be removed or set to "false" when validation passes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1305 (in markValid): Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` after removing aria-describedby references

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, keyboard focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the newly revealed navigation content. Keyboard users cannot immediately navigate the menu without manually tabbing into it.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation declares a focus trap variable but never initialises or activates it when opening. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background page content, breaking modal dialog expectations.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is created in the closed state, but the initialisation code never sets aria-expanded="false". The attribute only appears after the first toggle interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1835 (in init(), after `_trigger.addEventListener('keydown', _onTriggerKeydown);`): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement for filter results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count updates visually but no announcement is made to screen reader users via a live region. Users do not receive feedback that the filter has been applied or how many results are shown.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2445 (in applyFilters(), inside filterAnimation.run callback): Add after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`:
    ```
    liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found for ${_productFilterState.activeCategory === 'all' ? 'all categories' : _productFilterState.activeCategory}`);
    ```

issuesFound.title: pricing module missing live region announcement for billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, prices update visually with animation, but screen reader users receive no notification of the price change or billing period switch.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items expand and collapse without notifying screen reader users of the state change. While focus remains on the button, the content appearance is not announced, leaving users unaware if the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2836 (in open()): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);` after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });`
    Line 2856 (in close()): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);` after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });`

fpTitles: mobileNav open missing focus management

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button lacks an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element on initialisation. Screen reader users cannot identify which element is controlled by the toggle button.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init(), after selecting _drawer and _trigger: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not update aria-expanded when panels open or close. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded or collapsed state of the dropdown menus to users.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance(), after `inst.open = true;`: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`. In _closeInstance(), after `inst.open = false;`: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger missing aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ accordion item expands, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to true. Although the close function sets it to false, the missing update on open leaves screen readers unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open(), after `item.open = true;`: `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify which fields contain errors, violating WCAG 3.3.1 Error Identification.
issuesFound.fix: In srUtils.markInvalid(), after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the aria-invalid attribute is not removed or set to false. Screen readers may continue to report the field as invalid after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: In srUtils.markValid(), at the start of the function: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price updates are not announced to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology are unaware that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing.setPeriod(), after `_syncToggleState(period);`: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || `Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the updated result count is not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed how many products match their selected filters.
issuesFound.fix: In applyFilters(), inside the filterAnimation.run callback after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`: `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation menu closes, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users may not realize the menu has closed, particularly if focus management is not immediately apparent.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close(), before the closing brace: `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens and closes without updating the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2832: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;` and before `addClass(_drawer, ...)`
  Line 2854: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;` and before `removeClass(_drawer, ...)`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu panels open and close without updating aria-expanded on their trigger buttons, leaving screen readers unaware of the panel state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3124: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`
  Line 3142: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in _closeInstance after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing aria-expanded='true'
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion close function sets aria-expanded='false', but the open function fails to set aria-expanded='true', so screen readers do not announce when content expands.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3751: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;` and before `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but does not toggle aria-expanded when the suggestion list opens or closes, violating the ARIA combobox pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4083: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in _open function after `_searchState.open = true;`
  Line 4095: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in _close function after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count and category are not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that results have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4458: Insert `liveRegion.announce(s.activeCategory === (CONFIG.products.allCategoryValue || 'all') ? STRINGS.filterAll : STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` and before `_updateEmptyState(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates visible prices but does not announce the change or the new billing period to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4332: Insert `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` after `planDataParser.getAll().forEach(plan => _updatePlanCard(plan, period));`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes visible content without announcing the action to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3751: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.title) : item.title + ' expanded');` at end of open function
  Line 3775: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.title) : item.title + ' collapsed');` at end of close function

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation menu is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2832: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` at end of open function
  Line 2854: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` at end of close function

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu state changes are not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3124: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` at end of _openInstance
  Line 3142: Insert `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` at end of _closeInstance

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When file validation fails, aria-invalid is not set on the input element, so screen readers do not identify the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5255: Insert `setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `liveRegion.announceAssertive(...)` in validation failure block
  Line 5265: Insert `setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` after successful validation or when clearing errors

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set on page load, so screen readers don't know the menu is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2905: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_initialised = true;` in init function

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are not initialised with aria-expanded="false", so screen readers don't know panels are collapsed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3208: Insert `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` in register function

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input does not have aria-expanded="false" set initially, violating the ARIA combobox pattern for collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4158: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);` in init function

issuesFound.title: productFilter view mode toggle missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between grid and list view is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4725: Insert `liveRegion.announce(mode === 'list' ? 'Switched to list view' : 'Switched to grid view');` after `try { storage.set(...) }` block in _onViewToggle

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons function as a single-select tab list (with roving tabindex) but do not have aria-selected set to indicate which tab is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4625: Insert `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));` inside _syncTabAriaStates after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

fpTitles: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcement
dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialisation

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance and _closeInstance do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons fail to update their aria-expanded attribute when panels are opened or closed. This prevents screen reader users from perceiving the current state of the disclosure widgets.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._openInstance(): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` before `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...`
  Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance(): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` before `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ accordion item is expanded, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true". The close function correctly sets it to "false", but the open function omits this, leaving screen reader users unaware that the panel is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open(): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState does not update aria-pressed on billing period buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) function as a button group where one is always pressed. However, the code only toggles CSS classes and does not update the aria-pressed attribute to indicate which button is currently active/pressed.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid and markValid do not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, the aria-invalid attribute is never set to "true", and when cleared, it is not removed or set to "false". Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state of form fields to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line srUtils.markInvalid(): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`
  Line srUtils.markValid(): Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` after the aria-describedby cleanup logic.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialise aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have aria-expanded initialised to "false" when the page loads and the menu is closed. Screen readers cannot correctly announce the initial collapsed state of the navigation drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init(): After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);` and null checks, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialise aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered during initialisation, they are not given an initial aria-expanded value of "false". This leaves the attribute missing until the first interaction, causing incorrect state announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu.register(): After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init does not initialise aria-expanded on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input functions as a combobox that controls a suggestion listbox, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" on page load when the suggestion panel is closed. Screen readers cannot correctly announce the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line siteSearch.init(): After the event bindings, add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count and grid update, but there is no aria-live announcement to inform screen reader users how many products are now displayed or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters(): Inside the filterAnimation.run callback (after _animating = false), add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing period change
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually and the button states change, but there is no announcement to screen reader users confirming the new billing period or the updated prices.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod(): After `_syncToggleState(period);` add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput _onApply does not announce successful discount
issuesFound.explanation: When a discount code is successfully applied and prices are updated, there is no announcement to screen reader users confirming that the discount was applied and that prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line discountCodeInput._onApply(): Inside the `if (result.valid)` block, after `_applyDiscount(result.discountPct);` add `liveRegion.announce(result.message || 'Discount applied successfully');`

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips render does not announce chip changes
issuesFound.explanation: When filter chips are added or removed (reflecting the current active filters), the changes are not announced to screen reader users, leaving them unaware of the current filter state.
issuesFound.fix: Line activeFilterChips.render(): At the end of the function (before the closing brace), add `if (chips.length > 0) { liveRegion.announce(`Active filters: ${chips.map(c => c.label).join(', ')}`); }`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open does not activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing users to tab out of the menu into the background content. Additionally, focus is not moved into the drawer when it opens.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance does not return focus on outside click/escape
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown closes due to clicking outside the navigation or pressing Escape, focus is not returned to the trigger button, potentially leaving focus in an undefined location or on the document body.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._onDocumentFocusin: Change `_closeAll();` to `_closeAll(null, true);` (if modifying _closeAll to accept returnFocus) or explicitly track the currently open instance and call `_closeInstance(inst, true);`
  Line dropdownMenu._onPanelKeydown (Escape case): Change `_closeInstance(inst);` to `_closeInstance(inst, true);`

fpTitles: discountCodeInput _onApply does not announce successful discount
activeFilterChips render does not announce chip changes

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to false. Screen reader users are not informed that the navigation menu has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2336: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); immediately after _open = false;

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button does not have its aria-expanded attribute updated to false when the dropdown panel closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2556: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); immediately after inst.open = false;

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _close missing aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input does not set aria-expanded="false" when the suggestion panel closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3856: Add setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); before or after _searchState.open = false;

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a field as invalid, the function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1695: Add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearing
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing the invalid state from a field, the function removes the error description but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute. The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1706: Add removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID); or setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false'); after clearing the describedby references.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. The register function sets aria-haspopup but not the initial expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2605: Add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); in the register function after setting aria-haspopup.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search input combobox is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. The input receives role="combobox" and aria-haspopup="listbox" but lacks the initial expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3912: Add setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); after mounting the suggestion listbox.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens as a modal dialog but does not activate a focus trap. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer and access content behind the overlay, violating modal dialog accessibility patterns.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2324: Add _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); and _trap.activate(); after _lockScroll();

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus return on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the page and must navigate back to the trigger manually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2346: Add if (_trigger) _trigger.focus(); after _unlockScroll();

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filter criteria are applied, the product grid updates visually but no announcement is made to screen reader users about the number of results shown or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3558: Add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products filtered`); after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches);

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually with animation but screen reader users receive no announcement of the price change or the new billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4320: Add liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`); after updating the plan cards.

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator missing live region for auto-updates
issuesFound.explanation: Statistics animate automatically when scrolling into view, but the changing values are not announced to screen reader users, leaving them unaware of the updated content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4785: Add liveRegion.announce(`Statistic updated to ${cfg.prefix}${cfg.target}${cfg.suffix}`); inside onComplete callback (or use aria-live on the stat elements).

fpTitles: mobileNav missing focus return on close

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm error message ID mismatch breaks aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references 'newsletter-email-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup is passed the prop 'error' instead of 'errorMessage' and lacks the 'errorId' prop. Consequently, ErrorMessage is never rendered with the expected ID, so screen readers cannot announce the error message when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1350: <FormGroup errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email">

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name field error message not programmatically linked
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for the name field references 'contact-name-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup receives 'error' instead of 'errorMessage' and is missing the 'errorId' prop. The error message element is not rendered with the required ID, preventing screen reader users from hearing the error announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1420: <FormGroup errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined} label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email field error message not programmatically linked
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references 'contact-email-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup is not configured with errorId or errorMessage props. The error message is not associated with the input, so assistive technology users are not informed of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1440: <FormGroup errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject field error message not programmatically linked
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references 'contact-subject-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup uses the incorrect 'error' prop and omits 'errorId'. Screen readers cannot announce the error message because the referenced element does not exist in the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1460: <FormGroup errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined} label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message field error message not programmatically linked
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea references 'contact-message-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup is missing the errorId and errorMessage props. The error message is not rendered with the expected ID, breaking the connection for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1480: <FormGroup errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined} label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`}>

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute incorrectly placed on overlay
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is applied to the modal-overlay div instead of the dialog element with role="dialog". This causes assistive technologies to incorrectly identify the modal boundary, potentially allowing users to navigate outside the dialog while it is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1730: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}> (remove aria-modal="true" from this line) and Line 1732: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

Consequently, the ErrorMessage element with that id is never rendered into the DOM, leaving screen reader users unaware of field-specific validation errors.
