issuesFound.title: ContactForm name field error message broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for the name field sets aria-describedby="contact-name-error" when validation fails, but FormGroup lacks the errorId prop, so the error message element is not rendered. Screen reader users cannot perceive the validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1720: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute incorrectly placed on overlay element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div instead of the dialog element itself. This causes assistive technologies to potentially treat the backdrop as the modal root rather than the actual dialog, which may result in incorrect focus scope announcements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1890: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Line 1892: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

ContactForm name field error message broken aria-describedby reference
ContactForm email field error message broken aria-describedby reference
ContactForm subject field error message broken aria-describedby reference
ContactForm message field error message broken aria-describedby reference
Modal aria-modal attribute incorrectly placed on overlay element

issuesFound.title: ErrorMessage component receives children instead of message prop
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component in NewsletterForm is rendered with child text content instead of the required 'message' prop. The component implementation only renders the 'message' prop and ignores children, so the error text "Something went wrong..." is never displayed to users, including screen reader users who rely on this alert.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1370: <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" message="Something went wrong. Please try again in a moment." role="alert" />

issuesFound.title: FormGroup receives incorrect prop and missing errorId
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component for the email field in NewsletterForm uses the prop 'error' which does not exist in the component interface. It should use 'errorMessage' to pass the validation error text. Additionally, the 'errorId' prop is missing, which prevents the ErrorMessage from rendering with the correct id that matches the input's aria-describedby attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1360: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute placed on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The Modal component places aria-modal="true" on the overlay div instead of the dialog element. This incorrectly scopes the modal boundary and may cause assistive technologies to treat the overlay as the modal rather than the actual dialog content.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1590: Move aria-modal="true" from the overlay div to the role="dialog" div: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>

fpTitles: ErrorMessage component receives children instead of message prop
FormGroup receives incorrect prop and missing errorId
Modal aria-modal attribute placed on wrong element

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle button missing aria-pressed attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button toggles the isPlaying boolean state but does not communicate its pressed state (playing vs paused) to assistive technology using aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1358: <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" aria-label={isPlaying ? 'Pause background video' : 'Play background video'} aria-pressed={isPlaying} onClick={handleTogglePlay} className="hero-section__video-toggle">

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button controls the visibility of the answer panel via aria-controls but does not expose the panel's state with aria-expanded. Assistive technology users cannot tell if the item is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1690: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual billing toggle buttons act as a single-select switch where one option is always active. Neither button exposes its pressed state to assistive technology, so users cannot tell which billing period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1380: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>; Line 1395: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to style the field in error state, but it does not map this to aria-invalid. Screen-reader users are not notified when the input contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 785: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm broken aria-describedby references
issuesFound.explanation: The form inputs use aria-describedby to reference error message IDs (e.g., 'contact-name-error'), but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so ErrorMessage is never rendered and the IDs do not exist in the DOM. This creates broken ARIA references.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2230: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.error}>; Line 2250: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.error}>; Line 2270: <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.error}>; Line 2290: <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.error}>

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references 'newsletter-email-error' in aria-describedby when in error state, but FormGroup does not receive the errorId prop, so the ErrorMessage element is never rendered and the ID is missing from the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2120: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.error}>

issuesFound.title: ErrorMessage component usage incorrect (children vs message prop)
issuesFound.explanation: ErrorMessage expects a message prop to render content, but ContactForm and NewsletterForm pass the error text as children. This causes ErrorMessage to return null (since message is undefined), so the error is not displayed and any aria-describedby pointing to it references a non-existent element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2140: <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" message="Something went wrong. Please try again in a moment." role="alert" />; Line 2325: <ErrorMessage id="contact-server-error" message="Submission failed. Please check your connection and try again." role="alert" />

fpTitles: ContactForm broken aria-describedby references
ErrorMessage component usage incorrect (children vs message prop)

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button manages an open/close state (isOpen) and has aria-controls and aria-haspopup, but lacks aria-expanded. Screen readers cannot announce whether the submenu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 475: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation accordion sections use a boolean state (isExpanded) to show/hide content, but the trigger button lacks aria-expanded. Assistive technology cannot convey the expanded/collapsed state of each section.
issuesFound.fix: Line 545: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button element with className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger".

issuesFound.title: FAQ disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FaqItemComponent button toggles answer visibility using the isOpen prop but does not expose this state via aria-expanded. Screen reader users cannot tell whether an answer panel is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 830: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element with id={btnId}.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel navigation buttons missing accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The previous and next IconButton components contain only the characters "‹" and "›" with no aria-label prop, violating the IconButton interface requirement and leaving icon-only buttons without accessible names.
issuesFound.fix: Line 765: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial" to the first IconButton. Line 770: Add aria-label="Next testimonial" to the second IconButton.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons act as toggle buttons with an active state (activeFilter === tab.value) but lack aria-pressed. Screen readers cannot communicate which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 605: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to the button inside the FILTER_TABS map function.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly and annual billing buttons function as a toggle button group where one is always selected, but neither button has aria-pressed to indicate the selected state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 685: Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} to the first button. Line 692: Add aria-label={value === 'annual'} to the second button (and add aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}).

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link marked as current (item.current) uses a visual class (nav-link--current) but lacks aria-current="page", so screen readers cannot identify the current page in the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 515: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor element inside the DesktopNav map function.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link marked as current uses a visual class (mobile-nav__link--current) but lacks aria-current="page", preventing screen readers from announcing the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: Add aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} to the anchor element inside the MobileNav map function.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a <nav> element without an aria-label, creating an unnamed landmark region that appears in screen reader landmark lists without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 348: Add aria-label="Skip links" to the nav element.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection renders a <section> element without aria-label or aria-labelledby, despite containing a distinct page region with a visible heading (id="hero-heading").
issuesFound.fix: Line 625: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading" to the section element.

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar section contains a visible heading (id="stats-heading") but the section element lacks aria-labelledby, leaving the statistics region unnamed for screen reader landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: Add aria-labelledby="stats-heading" to the section element.

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard renders an <article> element containing a heading with id={headingId}, but the article lacks aria-labelledby, preventing screen readers from identifying the card by its plan name when navigating by region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 715: Add aria-labelledby={headingId} to the article element.

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicate list of integrations used for visual marquee looping lacks aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce the integration list twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 435: Add aria-hidden="true" to the second ul element (the clone containing items with key={`clone-${item.id}`}).

issuesFound.title: HeroSection trust badges group missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The div containing trust badges (WCAG, SOC 2, GDPR) represents a distinct group of related content but lacks an aria-label, making it unclear what the group represents when encountered by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 665: Add aria-label="Trust badges and certifications" to the div with className="hero-section__trust".

issuesFound.title: TextInput component not mapping invalid prop to aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` boolean prop to indicate error states but only uses it for CSS class styling. It does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element, so screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component not mapping invalid prop to aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an `invalid` boolean prop for styling error states but fails to map it to the aria-invalid attribute. Screen reader users are not informed when the field contains invalid data.

issuesFound.title: Select component not mapping invalid prop to aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component receives an `invalid` prop for visual error indication but does not apply aria-invalid to the select element. Assistive technology users cannot detect when the selection is invalid.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria-describedby references non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The email input uses aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" to link to its error message, but the FormGroup wrapper is not passed an errorId prop, so the ErrorMessage element is either not rendered or rendered without the expected id. Screen readers cannot announce error messages to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1180: <FormGroup
    label="Email address"
    htmlFor="newsletter-email"
    errorId="newsletter-email-error"
    error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
  >

issuesFound.title: StatsBar region missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: Section component not applying labelledby prop
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component accepts a `labelledby` prop but does not apply it to the rendered element via aria-labelledby. This prevents any section using this component from having an accessible name via its heading.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The required field indicator (asterisk) is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce "star" in addition to the visually hidden "required" text that follows. This creates redundant and potentially confusing announcements.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component accepts an `isLoading` prop to show a loading state but does not set aria-busy="true" on the button element. Screen reader users are not notified that the button is busy processing an action.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation items check for `item.current` to apply styling, but do not communicate the current page state to assistive technology via aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which page is currently active in the navigation.

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar duplicate list not hidden from AT
